name: kinetic-typography
description: Kinetic typography animation with 4 sequentially-revealed words/phrases, each using a distinct entrance style (slide, scale-overshoot, opacity+position, text animator per-character). Designed for title cards, lyric videos, product launch statements, and event openers. Bold modern typography on a near-black background with a Neon/Cyber Pop palette.

word_1.description: First word — slides in from the left

word_2.description: Second word — scales in with overshoot bounce

word_3.description: Third word — drops in with opacity + Y position

word_4.description: Fourth word — per-character text animator reveal

font_family.description: Font family — Inter, Space Grotesk, or Outfit all work well

color_primary.description: Primary neon color [r,g,b] 0-1 — default is Neon Magenta #FF2DAA

color_secondary.description: Secondary neon color [r,g,b] 0-1 — default is Electric Cyan #00A3FF

color_accent.description: Accent color [r,g,b] 0-1 — default is Electric Violet #7B2CFF

bg_color.description: Background near-black [r,g,b] 0-1 — default is Cyber Base #0A0A0F

steps.note: Near-black base #0A0A0F (cool blue cast) maximises neon vibrancy — never use pure black for neon palettes per the research doc's application rules. Store returned ID as $compId.
Explicit background solid ensures the near-black renders cleanly with no comp transparency bleeding through on export.

120pt Black weight at 1080p matches the hero/main title scale (80–160px per research). All-caps tracking at +150 per the critical rule: all-caps at 80px+ requires minimum +150 tracking. Neon Magenta #FF2DAA as primary color. Anchor centered for symmetric slide animation.
Word 1 is visible for the full 6s comp — it slides in early and holds as the kinetic background anchor.

Slide in from -300x (off-screen left) to center. 0.4s duration at 0.7× Tech/SaaS multiplier of a standard 583ms line reveal = ~408ms ≈ 0.4s. Element enters from off-screen so the easing should start at full speed and ease into the landing (backwards-J curve).
easeOutExpo equivalent via ease_out — correct for off-screen entrances: start fast, coast into landing. Snappy, punchy motion matching the Neon/Cyber Pop energy.

110pt — slightly smaller than Word 1 for visual rhythm variation (±20-30% per research timing principle). Electric Cyan #00A3FF as secondary color, centered on the comp's visual vertical midpoint for eye-catching placement.
Stagger: Word 2 starts at 1.0s. Research stagger target: 0.8–1.2s per word for kinetic typography; 1.0s is the natural midpoint.

Scale-in with overshoot: 0→115%→95%→100%. Manual keyframe overshoot pattern from research: overshoot to 110-120%, pull back to 95-98%, settle at 100%. Decreasing intervals (0.3s, 0.12s, 0.08s) mirroring real spring physics decay. 'Standard motion graphics' overshoot = 10-15% above target.
easeInOutCubic on scale gives organic acceleration into the overshoot and smooth spring-back. Appropriate for between-two-on-screen-positions motion per the easing cheatsheet.
100pt with Electric Violet #7B2CFF — accent color for this word. Positioned in the lower third of the typographic stack, creating a cascading vertical reading rhythm.
Stagger: Word 3 starts at 2.0s — consistent 1.0s between each word for clear rhythmic pacing.

Opacity fade from 0→100 over 0.35s — slightly longer than the 0.3s snappy benchmark to feel a touch more weighted than the scale pop. Opacity alone would be weak, so we combine with position drop below.
Fast opacity arrival (ease_out) for an immediate presence hit — text 'snaps on' with character.

Drop-in position: falls 60px (Y: 620→680) as opacity fades in — simulates gravity arriving with velocity. 60px / 1080px ≈ 5.6% of frame height: subtle enough to be energetic without being cartoonish. Matches the 'opacity + position' entrance requirement.
easeOutQuint feel on position — fast deceleration at rest point reinforces weight and physicality of the drop.

120pt off-white #F5F5F5 — pure white is avoided per neon palette rules. Placed at the top of the frame (y=270, in the upper third) to create non-linear reading order: top → middle → lower — deliberate, dynamic, not a simple top-to-bottom list. This word uses a text animator for character-by-character effect.
Stagger: Word 4 starts at 3.0s — all 4 words visible from 3s onward for maximum impact hold before comp ends.
Text Animator with Opacity + Position properties enables per-character stagger — the most professional text reveal technique in motion graphics. Each character cascades in independently.
Start state: each character at 0% opacity and -40px Y (above final position). The animator defines the FROM state; range selector interpolates toward the default (100% opacity, [0,0] position).

Range selector sweeps from 0→100% over 0.5s — at 29.97fps this is ~15 frames. Research table: 'word-by-word text' stagger at 3-6 frames per word. With 5 characters spanning 0.5s = 3 frames per character @ 29.97fps — right in the snappy range. Smooth shape creates soft character-by-character cascade (not hard cut-on).

Decorative accent line under Word 1 (THINK). Secondary action — starts after Word 1 is established. This shape acts as a visual underline accent, reinforcing the primary element per research Principle 7: secondary action supports, never competes.

Thin 3px horizontal line (400px wide, same Neon Magenta as Word 1). 3px thickness: fine enough to be minimal, bold enough to read at 1080p. 400px width ≈ the approximate text width of 'THINK' at 120pt with tracking 150.

Wipe-in scale on X axis only: 0%→100% X while Y stays constant at 100%. This is the 'Accent Bar wipe' technique from the lower-third template. Starts at t=0.4 (exactly when Word 1 finishes its slide-in) — secondary action timing rule: starts when primary action ends.
easeOut on the wipe keeps it snappy — the line 'slaps in' from left to right, matching the kinetic energy of the composition.

Arrow accent in Mint/Cyan #00FFD5 — a fourth color pulled from the Cyber Pop palette for energy. Positioned at rule-of-thirds right column (x≈1700) in the vertical center, creating compositional balance against the left-weighted word stack. Adds typographic punctuation and visual energy.
Arrow appears mid-sequence at 2.5s, bridging the gap between Word 3 (2.0s) and Word 4 (3.0s). Acts as a visual 'beat' between major elements.
Fast 0.15s opacity pop for the arrow — micro-interaction timing (150ms per research table). Quick enough to read as punctuation, not a heavy entrance.

params.expression: var t = time - 2.5; var ox = Math.sin(t * 2) * 8; [1700 + ox, 540];

steps.note: Subtle horizontal wiggle expression (ES3-compatible): arrow oscillates ±8px horizontally at 2Hz after it appears. Gives it living energy without being distracting. Amplitude 8px is within the 5-20px organic range. Uses Math.sin for smooth, continuous drift — no wiggle() needed.

Word 1 exit: fades out at the very end (5.5→5.85s, 0.35s). Exits are 20-30% faster than entrances — entrance was 0.4s, exit is 0.35s. Staggered exit would be ideal for v2 but single opacity exit here keeps the example clean.
easeIn on exit opacity — accelerates toward invisible, no lingering. For off-screen exits: 'accelerate the whole way out' per easing rules.
Motion blur on Word 1's slide-in dramatically enhances the kinetic feel — fast-moving text smears realistically. Essential for kinetic typography credibility.
Motion blur on Word 2 — even the scale pop benefits from motion blur, giving the scale-up a physical, photographic quality.
Motion blur on Word 3 (drop-in) — the vertical fall reads much more dynamically with blur on the Y-axis movement.
Shutter angle 180° is the cinematic standard (equivalent to 1/48s at 24fps — the 'film look'). 16 samples per frame provides smooth blur without render performance issues on text layers.
Add to render queue after all animation is complete. Use H.264 for web delivery or ProRes 4444 for compositing.
